finding: Objection (Coupling): The plan proposes a monolithic constants/index.ts with 14 URL constants, 6 timeout values, 5 storage keys, and 4 cache limits — yet only 2 values (DESKTOP_FEEDBACK_URL at 3 sites, DEFAULT_URL at 2 sites) are actually duplicated. The remaining 27 values are single-use within their owning modules. Extracting them to a shared file creates a hub-and-spoke dependency pattern that couples ~8 files to one module for no duplication-reduction benefit. Per AGENTS.md: "Do not extract single-use helpers preemptively." This increases coupling without commensurate payoff.
repair_path: Split the constants work: (1) extract only duplicated values (DESKTOP_FEEDBACK_URL, DEFAULT_URL) into constants/index.ts. (2) Leave TIMEOUTS local to server-health.ts (they're already cleanly scoped). (3) Leave STORAGE_KEYS local to their owning modules. (4) Keep CACHE_LIMITS, SAMPLE_SIZE, and single-use URL constants in their original files until a second use appears. This reduces the coupling footprint from 8 importing files to 4 at most.

finding: Objection (Surface area): The plan proposes creating a `types/ipc.ts` module described only as "Shared IPC types module that re-exports types for the renderer to use" — but the actual IPC/Window.api type boundary is already declared in `app.tsx:70-87`. Existing types live in `types/agent.ts` and `types/mcp.ts`. The plan does not specify: what types go in ipc.ts? How does it relate to the existing Window.api interface? What re-exports are needed? Creating this file without a spec is undefined scope creep that risks either (a) dumping everything into one file, or (b) creating a file that nothing uses.
repair_path: Defer types/ipc.ts creation. Add a concrete spec in the plan: list the exact types, their definitions, and which files import them. Alternatively, extend the existing Window.api interface with any missing methods (already partially done in app.tsx:83) and add type aliases for complex parameter shapes to the relevant types/agent.ts or types/mcp.ts file.

finding: Objection (Convergence): The plan proposes an `ErrorFallback` component described as "a themed error fallback that shows error message, retry button, and report button" — but `packages/app/src/pages/error.tsx` already exports a production-grade `ErrorPage` component (371 lines) that does all of this with localized error formatting, update checking, Sentry recording, and debug log export. Creating a parallel ErrorFallback duplicates existing functionality and forces future engineers to maintain two error display paths. This increases the revision risk because the two implementations will inevitably diverge.
repair_path: Remove ErrorFallback from the plan. Instead, have the per-route ErrorBoundary fallback render the existing ErrorPage component (imported from `./pages/error.tsx`), possibly wrapping it with a retry/close handler. The ErrorPage already takes `{ error: unknown }`, which matches the ErrorBoundary fallback signature exactly.

finding: Informational note: The plan's constants scope is incomplete for single-use URLs that are still spread across the codebase. `https://opencode.ai/favicon.svg` appears in `packages/app/src/pages/layout/helpers.ts:61`, `https://opencode.ai/docs` in `packages/app/src/desktop-menu.ts:206`, `https://opencode.ai/docs/providers/#custom-provider` in `components/dialog-custom-provider.tsx:186`, `https://opencode.ai/docs/themes/` in `components/settings-general.tsx:519`, and `https://opencode.ai/zen` in `components/dialog-connect-provider.tsx:429`. If the plan wants a central constants module, these should be cataloged too — or the plan should explicitly declare these "not in scope" as it did for i18n files.
repair_path: Either add these to the constants/index.ts scope or note them explicitly as deferred. Leaving them unmentioned creates an expectation gap.

finding: Observability note: The ErrorBoundary enhancement is the highest-value change in the plan. The existing `app.tsx` wraps the entire app in a single `<ErrorBoundary>` (line 172), which means any route crash takes down the whole app. Adding per-route boundaries is a meaningful crash-isolation improvement. The `AppErrorBoundary` component correctly integrates Sentry. No issues with this work item itself — the plan scope concerns are about the other 66% of the changes (constants extraction, types/ipc.ts, ErrorFallback).
repair_path: Keep the ErrorBoundary work as-designed. Consider executing it as a separate lane from the constants work so the two can be reviewed and published independently.
